title: Fixing Invalid Git Cherry-Picked Commit Hash Validation

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate a cherry-picked commit hash in Git. A valid commit hash must be exactly 40 characters long and consist only of hexadecimal digits (0-9, a-f, A-F). If the input is valid, return `true`; otherwise, return `false`. Note that this function should be case-insensitive.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the cherry-picked commit hash
statement.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether the commit hash is valid
statement.notes: This function should handle both uppercase and lowercase hexadecimal digits.

examples.explanation: The input is a valid commit hash.
The input contains a non-hexadecimal digit.
The input has an invalid length.
The input is a valid commit hash regardless of case.
